Title: 10 teens arrested over assault on student in Tawau
Article:
TAWAU: Police have detained 10 teenage boys over the assault of a student who was on his way to Friday prayers in Merotai Besar last week.
Tawau acting police chief Superintendent Wilynton Enchana Watt said the suspects, aged between 14 and 17, were arrested at 2pm yesterday.
Wilynton said preliminary investigations showed the 14-year-old victim and three friends were taking a shortcut to the mosque along Jalan Sri Delima, Merotai Besar, when they were approached by a group of students.
The group questioned the victim about the whereabouts of two other people.
"After the victim said he did not know where they were, the group allegedly attacked and beat him up.
"The victim's three friends were also believed to have been assaulted by the group," he said in a statement.
He said the victim suffered injuries and pain to his face, head, chest, and ribs, while his three friends were also injured.
They later lodged a police report, and the teens were arrested.
"The case is being investigated under Section 147 of the Penal Code for rioting, and further investigations are ongoing to determine the cause of the incident," he said.
Rioting is punishable up to two years in jail, a fine, or both.
The case gained public attention after a video recording of
the assault
went viral on social media.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RoS mulls mandatory audits, new mechanism to handle public donations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Registrar of Societies (RoS) is planning to introduce a new audit mechanism to tighten financial governance among registered societies, including non-governmental organisations (NGOs).
The initiative aims to curb the misuse of public donations following several high-profile cases, as well as review the requirement for mandatory audits for societies that manage large sums of public funds.
With cases of fund misappropriation on the rise, RoS is studying various improvements, including tightening the regulation of public donation collections and ensuring accountability, transparency and integrity among registered societies in fund management.
RoS chairman Datuk Mohd Zulfikar Ahmad said the department was committed to strengthening the regulatory framework for societies on an ongoing basis, including proposals to tighten financial reporting requirements and introduce an audit mechanism in line with current needs.
"Any proposal to make audits mandatory for certain categories of societies, including a requirement to submit annual financial statements, can be considered within the context of amendments to the Societies Act 1966," he said.
Currently, societies are governed under the Societies Act 1966, which is undergoing review.
RoS is calling for stiffer penalties against societies found to have misused public funds, following several cases where NGOs were exposed for misappropriating charitable donations and zakat funds meant for the needy.
Recent enforcement action against several societies uncovered NGOs collecting large sums and purchasing luxury goods for personal use.
This year alone, at least seven NGOs are being investigated for collecting public donations and charitable funds under false pretences.
He said current penalties for offences under the Societies Act are too lenient, with fines capped at RM5,000, which is no longer commensurate with the scale of the crime.
He also called for harsher punishment for embezzlement offences involving societies, particularly in cases where individuals found guilty are barred from holding positions of trust within registered societies.
Under Section 53 of the Societies Act, offences carry penalties of a fine not exceeding RM5,000 for registered societies. Section 9A(1)(a) of the same Act requires the management of registered societies to be declared to RoS.
"Anyone convicted should be barred from being appointed to any position in any registered society. Nonetheless, individuals implicated could still hold posts in other societies as there is no integrated criminal record system," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Sarawak pet shop accused of leaving cats out in the sun for 'sympathy buys'
Article:
KUCHING: A pet shop in Kota Samarahan, Sarawak, has sparked outrage online after a couple claimed it left cats out in the sun to attract 'sympathy buys' from passers-by.
The incident gained traction after Threads user @muhdnhaffizzul posted two videos showing
cats in cages
outside the premises, alleging that the animals had been deliberately exposed to extreme heat to elicit sympathy from potential buyers.
He and his wife said they noticed a cage containing Bengal cats placed outside the shop.
However, they later discovered that one of the cats had died.
"There used to be two Bengal cats. When we asked where the other one was, an employee told us it had died from an illness," he wrote.
"I wonder what kind of 'illness' it could have been, if not from being left to roast outside in the heat?
"We could see one cat barely enduring the heat, while the other was drinking water non-stop."
The couple said they confronted the shop workers.
"If you keep leaving the cats outside like that, it won't be long before another one dies because it is too hot. Take them inside the shop," they told the staff.
However, @muhdnhaffizzul claimed their pleas were ignored.
The couple said they had lodged a report with the Sarawak Department of Veterinary Services (DVS).
In the comments section, user @meira_edora said she feared the cats were dehydrated.
Meanwhile, @h.a.z.w.a.n.i.o.t.h.m.a.n wrote: "Poor thing. The Persian cat with thick fur is not meant to be outside. Put it in the shop."
Another user, @khu_ningrat, wrote: "Please DVS, enact laws against animal abuse. This is abuse."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: JS-SEZ delay risks Johor's economic momentum, says exco
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Delaying the launch of the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) master plan any further could undermine Johor's economic momentum and investor confidence, Lee Ting Han said today.
The caretaker Johor Investment, Trade, Consumer Affairs and Human Resources Committee chairman threw his support behind caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's call for the immediate rollout of the blueprint, saying Johor could not afford to lose
the momentum
built over the past two years.
"I fully support the menteri besar's call for the JS-SEZ master plan and action plan to be announced without any further delay. Johor cannot lose the momentum built through years of hard work," he told the New Straits Times.
He said Johor had proven its economic strength, recording the country's fastest gross domestic product (GDP) growth of 6.4 per cent in 2024 and 8.0 per cent last year, while attracting a record RM110 billion in approved investments.
"If Johor has achieved the nation's highest economic growth and investment even without the JS-SEZ master plan, imagine what more we can achieve once the entire JS-SEZ ecosystem is fully implemented," he said.
He described the master plan as a critical roadmap that would provide certainty to investors, coordinate infrastructure development, strengthen talent planning and align government agencies, warning that every delay gives competing investment destinations an opportunity to gain ground.
"This was not achieved by chance. It was driven by clear leadership, consistent policies, close collaboration with the private sector and strong cooperation between the state and federal governments.
"If Johor has already achieved the nation's highest economic growth and investment figures even before the JS-SEZ master plan is implemented, imagine what more can be achieved once the entire JS-SEZ ecosystem is fully operational."
Lee said speed had become a competitive advantage in an increasingly challenging global economy, warning that every delay allowed rival investment destinations to seize opportunities that should belong to Johor and Malaysia.
He also called for continued professional and results-oriented cooperation between the state government and the federal administration despite any differences in opinion.
"Our common goal must remain the same — ensuring Johor continues to prosper and Malaysia continues to thrive.
"Johor has proven it can lead the nation's economy. Now we must ensure this momentum is not only maintained, but accelerated," he said.
Lee was responding to a disagreement between the caretaker state leadership and the federal government over the delayed launch.
Onn Hafiz has argued that the blueprint, originally expected last year before being rescheduled, should be
launched immediately
as it is a Malaysian government document that does not require approval from Singapore.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Venezuela quake death toll rises to nearly 3,000
Article:
CARACAS: Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes killed nearly 3,000, updated official figures showed on Saturday, as international rescue teams began winding down search operations for survivors in the rubble of the disaster.
Fatalities jumped by more than 300 from Friday to 2,954, following the June 24 disaster that has left thousands homeless in the streets and sheltering in camps.
Tens of thousands more are still reported missing. The government has not given any estimates, but the United Nations has estimated as many as 50,000 are unaccounted for following the 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude shocks.
One of Latin America's worst earthquake disasters hit hardest in the coastal La Guaira area north of the capital Caracas, where scores of residential complexes were flattened.
Ten days after the double shocks, which came just 38 seconds apart, rescue teams are starting to wrap up searches for survivors while families still try to recover bodies of loved ones from the wreckage.
The critical window for rescues in disasters like earthquakes usually ends after 72 hours, though a few people have been found alive this week.
In an apparent sign that rescue missions were closing up, interim President Delcy Rodriquez held a ceremony to hand out medals to international teams, including their rescue dogs.
Venezuela is experiencing "a profound grief gripping our people, where families still hold out hope of finding loved ones alive, people who have lost everything," Rodriquez said.
International disaster teams, including some US squads, and some South American teams were starting to finish up rescue operations, their members said on Saturday.
The Los Angeles County fire department rescue team is ending operations after its latest searches showed no signs of life, and teams from Florida and Virginia were packing up to leave this weekend, they said.
Many Venezuelans have expressed anger at what they see as their government's slow response to the disaster, saying families spent initial hours digging out loved ones themselves before international teams arrived.
Rodriguez has defended her government's response, saying thousands of troops and officials had been dispatched.
In La Guaira, workers with heavy machinery on Saturday were starting to knock down collapsed structures while in others, families were still trying to remove bodies of loved ones for funerals.
"We're still working, still searching for bodies. We're still going. It hasn't been easy," said Venezuelan volunteer Francisco Sasquia helping dig out a collapsed residence.
"We found two bodies that have already been released to their families."
- Economic fallout -
The United Nations has estimated that the twin earthquakes caused $6.7 billion in physical damage, equivalent to six percent of GDP for Venezuela, an oil exporting country.
Even before last week's disaster struck, Venezuela had struggled with decades of economic crisis and political upheaval that undermined its infrastructure and health services.
Maiquetia international airport in La Guaira, which serves Caracas, was also damaged in the quakes.
It has reopened partially to allow humanitarian flights to land, but commercial flights are still suspended.
"We are in touch with some international partners, countries that wil help in restoring the Maiquetia international airport," Rodriguez said at another event.
"A plan will be ready next week."
For Víctor Colivert, the most important thing now was staying by the side of his nephew's body, recovered from a building's wreckage and now in a black body bag.
He fears losing it in the chaos. His family prevented forensic workers from taking the body away.
"If I have to go to China, to wherever, but I'm not leaving him alone," he said.
"I'm going with him." — AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mydin MD rejects perception of strong Bumiputera funding support
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Mydin Mohamed Holdings managing director Datuk Dr Ameer Ali Mydin has dismissed the perception that Bumiputera entrepreneurs enjoy extensive government support.
He said meaningful financing was largely unavailable once businesses outgrow the micro-enterprise level.
Ameer, who is  vice-president of the Malaysia Retailers Association, said government assistance was largely limited to small loans for B40 groups and micro-, small- and medium-scale enterprises.
"People say the government provides financing. Show me what kind. Usually, they're referring to Amanah Ikhtiar giving RM5,000 or Mara (Majlis Amanah Rakyat) giving RM10,000.
"That isn't real business financing. That's giving money for political advantage," he told the
New Straits Times
.
"There's no government funding to help Bumiputera businesses move from one level to the next. Most funding is at the B40 or micro-SME level.
"That's good for getting them started, but once they climb a couple of rungs, there's no avenue for growth."
He also rejected the notion that Bumiputera entrepreneurs enjoyed an unfair advantage, saying they continued to face structural barriers in obtaining financing.
He added that government assistance should focus on building sustainable Bumiputera enterprises instead of distributing small grants.
"Giving RM5,000 or RM10,000 is just politics. It's not real help.
"If they really wanted to help, there would be a structured programme to develop Bumiputera businesses."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US turns 250 with Trump center stage
Article:
WASHINGTON: The United States celebrated its 250th birthday on Saturday with parades, flyovers and fireworks to come, as President Donald Trump stamped his mark on the landmark anniversary in a deeply divided nation.
But weather threatened to spoil Trump's super-sized party in Washington, as tens of thousands who had gathered on the National Mall hours ahead of a campaign rally-style address by the president were ordered to evacuate due to an approaching thunderstorm that followed hours of searing heat.
While many streamed toward exits, chaos broke out as others refused to leave or tried to surge back in, shouting "charge!" and "Trump! Trump!"
"We are evacuating! Move!" officers shouted to the thousands of holdouts, ranging from elderly visitors to families with infants. It was not clear when the site would reopen.
Trump had organised hours of military flyovers and an unusual campaign-style political rally for July Fourth, the anniversary of the 1776 signing of the country's Declaration of Independence.
The finale was a fireworks show that was touted as the biggest ever.
"Despite the heat, which isn't as bad as predicted, the crowds in DC are INCREDIBLE!" Trump posted to Truth Social earlier Saturday, as temperatures soared to 101F (38C) in the capital.
A swath of the eastern US was sweltering in a heat wave, with 160 million Americans under extreme weather warnings, according to the National Weather Service.
Heat wreaked havoc on the parades, block parties and barbecues that traditionally mark July Fourth.
Earlier, Washington's parade was cancelled as the "feels like" temperature soared to 107F.
"The event and what it means to our country, it inspires you," said Randy Cole, 62, a retired civil servant attending festivities in Washington.
"Experiencing a little heat is a lot less than what a lot of people sacrifice to give us this freedom in this awesome country."
Trump, 80, said he was undeterred by the smothering heat and promised to make a "really long speech — just to show that I can do anything" — though the speech was slated for cooler hours after sunset.
Patrick Thompson, a teacher in the Washington suburb of Alexandria, Virginia, said he would enjoy the day — but not at the national fireworks show.
"We still want to celebrate the country and be patriotic and it's exciting," Thompson, a father of two teenagers, told AFP.
"But it's so weird because it's got this...strange mar over it because of Trump."
- 'Renewed attack' -
Trump visited the iconic Mount Rushmore monument in South Dakota on Friday for an address under the stony gaze of his legendary predecessors George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, Abraham Lincoln and Theodore Roosevelt.
While lauding US exceptionalism, he also said America's identity was under "renewed attack" from domestic "radicals and extremists," saving particular ire for a "resurgence of the communist menace."
It is a theme that the Republican leader has repeatedly hammered home recently, after the anti-establishment left of the Democratic Party won a string of US primary victories.
Deep divisions were on full display Saturday near Washington's Capitol Hill, where masked men — some of them carrying Confederate flags and others sporting logos of the white supremacist Patriot Front — gathered to shout, "Reclaim America!"
Pope Leo XIV — the Catholic Church's first US pope, who has sparred with Trump over his immigration crackdown — used the occasion to say his vision of the American dream includes an inclusive society.
"Defending human life also includes welcoming, protecting and assisting immigrants, whose hopes, sacrifices and contribution have formed part of the history of this country from its very beginning," the Chicago-born pontiff said.
- Celebration and reflection -
For Americans, the 250th anniversary offers a moment for reflection as well as celebration.
A Quinnipiac University Poll showed 61 per cent of Americans thought the US was not living up to the ideals stated in the Declaration of Independence — though most Republicans think it does, and most Democrats think it doesn't.
Outside Washington, New York was hosting an international parade of tall ships, with flyovers and a massive fireworks display.
In Philadelphia, visitors lined up in the heat to see the Liberty Bell and Independence Hall, where the Declaration of Independence was signed.
For Loselie Weber, who was visiting Washington from Texas, the holiday inspires gratitude.
"As an immigrant — a legal immigrant — I came when I was seven years old, and I've been thankful, very thankful to be able to have the privilege of living here and the freedom that it has offered me," Weber told AFP. — AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Anwar steps up PH campaign momentum, hits 8 state constituencies today
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today stepped up the coalition's campaign momentum for the Johor state election with a packed tour across eight constituencies in the northern part of the state, aimed at strengthening public support for its candidates and machinery.
Today's tour will see the prime minister engaging with voters in Layang-Layang, Senggarang, Semerah, Bukit Naning, Pemanis, Gambir, Serom, and Palong Timur, which fall under the Buloh Kasap state legislative assembly seat, according to a post on his Facebook page.
Anwar begins his agenda as early as 8.55am with the 'PMX Santai Sarapan Bersama Masyarakat DUN Layang-Layang' breakfast programme at Warung Pak Din in Kampung Sahri, before attending a meet-and-greet session in Senggarang at 10am.
He is then scheduled to join a 'Kenduri Rakyat' (community feast) at Vila Kemboja in Semerah at 11am, followed by a 'Temu Anwar' session and the launch of Bukit Naning volunteers at a restaurant in Muar at 12.15pm.
In the afternoon, Anwar is set to attend the 'Jelajah Harapan Felda Bersama PMX' programme at Dataran Putra Palong Timur 2 at 3.10pm, followed by a meet-and-greet session with residents of Pemanis at Kampung Jawa at 5pm.
The tour continues into the evening with a meet-and-greet session in Gambir at Medan Selera Sin Tian Di, Bukit Gambir at 8pm.
Anwar is scheduled to conclude the day's programme with the 'Jelajah Johor Ke Depan, Undi Harapan' event in Serom at the compound of Pasaraya Mega in Sungai Mati at 9pm.
Yesterday, Anwar stepped up the campaign, now entering its second week, through a packed tour of seven programmes in the area.
For the upcoming state polls, PH is fielding candidates in all 56 state seats, comprising 20 candidates from PKR, 19 from Amanah and 17 from DAP.
Overall, the Johor state election sees a total of 172 candidates contesting, with polling day set for July 11 and early voting on July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit forecast to trade at RM4.05-RM4.07 against US dollar next week
Article:
Bernama
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit is expected to trade between RM4.05 and RM4.07 against the US dollar next week, with market attention likely to centre on the United States Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) minutes and Bank Negara Malaysia's (BNM) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting.
Both events fall on Thursday, July 9.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said traders, investors and economists would be watching the FOMC minutes closely for clues on the direction of US interest rates, especially given the absence of forward-looking guidance at the June FOMC meeting.
"We believe BNM will keep the overnight policy rate (OPR) steady at 2.75 per cent as there are no compelling reasons to change the prevailing monetary stance," he told Bernama.
Mohd Afzanizam added that the US dollar/ringgit pair may attempt to break the RM4.05 level next week.
On a Friday-to-Friday basis, the ringgit appreciated to 4.0690/0735 against the US dollar from 4.0860/0910 a week earlier.
The local currency traded mostly higher against a basket of major currencies during the week.
It rose against the Japanese yen to 2.5253/5281 from 2.5283/5316, and advanced vis-à-vis the euro to 4.6553/6606 from 4.6605/6662 previously.
However, it slid against the British pound to 5.4325/4385 from 5.4013/4079 last week.
The ringgit was mostly firmer against Asean currencies.
It strengthened against the Singapore dollar to 3.1521/1558 from 3.1581/1625, strengthened against the Indonesian rupiah to 226.5/226.8 from 227.9/228.3, and improved against the Philippine peso to 6.62/6.63 from 6.66/6.67 last Friday.
Meanwhile, it eased against the Thai baht to 12.2782/2966 from 12.2445/264 previously.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: North Korea's Kim oversees latest naval weapons tests
Article:
SEOUL: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un oversaw weapons tests this week of the 5,000-tonne destroyer Kang Kon, including cruise missile launches and "electronic warfare means", state media reported Sunday.
The naval vessel was notably the one that tipped over last year during its launch ceremony, but later repaired.
The tests took place on Friday, the state-run Korean Central News Agency (KCNA) said, less than two weeks after another 5,000-tonne destroyer, the Choe Hyon entered military service.
During that commissioning ceremony, Kim vowed to equip his navy with nuclear weapons and develop new 10,000-tonne warships.
After the weapons test on Friday, Kim "gave an instruction to complete the trial process of the destroyer in a responsible manner and commission it for the Navy within two months," KCNA said.
A photo released by KCNA shows Kim, wearing a yellow hat, watching a weapons test from a coastal observation point, flanked by officials.
Another photo shows Kang Kon firing a missile at sea, with a plume of smoke rising from the launch.
The Kang Kon suffered a setback in May last year when it partially capsized after tipping into the water during its launch, damaging the vessel in full view of Kim.
He blamed the accident on "absolute carelessness" and "irresponsibility", ordering that those responsible be held accountable.
Pyongyang has repeatedly declared itself an "irreversible" nuclear state since a 2019 summit between Kim and US counterpart Donald Trump in Hanoi collapsed over the scope of denuclearisation and sanctions relief.
North Korea remains technically at war with the South because the neighbours' 1950-53 conflict ended in an armistice rather than a peace treaty.
The South Korean navy runs more than 10 ships over 5,000 tonnes compared to the North's two.
While reiterating the need to speed up the North's "war deterrent", Kim "affirmed that we will demonstrate through more obvious actions our political will and determination to have an absolute power," KCNA said, without giving more details.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Renggam not a 'dead town', say residents
Article:
KLUANG: By dusk, many of Renggam's shopfronts already have their shutters pulled down, traffic begins to thin and many of the town's youths are kilometres away earning a living in Johor Baru, Singapore or Kuala Lumpur.
To some, that is enough to label the Kluang district town  "dead".
But after spending a day speaking to residents, traders and business owners, the
New Sunday Times
found a more nuanced picture.
It is a town that has  lost some of its economic vibrancy, yet remains a close-knit community; one  whose residents believe  has simply been overshadowed by neighbouring towns rather than abandoned altogether.
The debate was sparked by a recent media report describing Renggam as a "dead village".
It suggested establishing higher education institutions or colleges in Renggam to attract new residents and revitalise the local economy.
Businessman Rahim Puari, 56,  said it was misleading to call Renggam a dead town.
Having operated a business in the town for several months, he said many claims  on social media were inaccurate, including assertions that Renggam lacked  basic facilities.
"Renggam is  a strategic route connecting Kluang, Kulai, Bandar Tenggara and Johor Baru.
"Whenever there's congestion on the highway, many motorists use this road as a shortcut.
"So I don't agree with the dead town label."
Rahim acknowledged that job opportunities remained limited, but said the issue was not unique to Renggam.
Long-time resident Awlaaeliakani, 67, said the town seemed quiet was  due to the closure of factories that once sustained the local economy.
"There used to be rubber factories and other industries here.
"When they closed,  people left to work elsewhere. That's why they  say the town is dead."
But the cendol seller said the town's tranquillity was  one of its greatest strengths.
A civil servant, who wished to be known only as Liza, moved to Renggam from Melaka and has worked in the town for three years.
She believed the issue was less about the number of people, but the lack of attractions.
She said compared with nearby Simpang Renggam, there was little to encourage families or youth people to spend time in the town.
"Simpang Renggam has almost everything — restaurants, shops and places for families.
"Here, people come to send their children to school, run errands and then leave.
"There aren't many recreational spots or reasons to stay."
Convenience store operator Tham Wen Jye, 45, perhaps best illustrated the dilemma facing many families in Renggam.
After working in Singapore for several years, he returned home about five years ago to take over the family business following his father's retirement.
His siblings still live away. One lives in Kuala Lumpur and the other in Singapore.
"There aren't many jobs here. I came back because someone had to take care of the family business and my parents."
Located in Kluang district, Renggam has a population of more than 17,000 and was selected for the government's Bandar Belia Malaysia initiative, a youth focused township concept aimed at integrating employment, entrepreneurship, affordable housing and recreational facilities.
Despite the initiative, residents said the town needed stronger economic catalysts to retain the younger generation.
While opinions remain divided over whether Renggam deserves its "dead town" reputation, many residents agree on one thing: the town needs more investments and jobs if it is to regain its former vibrancy.
As Rahim put it: "Maybe we're quieter than before. But quiet doesn't mean dead."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM: Development in Johor needs to be balanced
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Johor needed development that was more balanced between rural areas, villages and the urban poor, as well as continued implementation of high-impact projects.
He said the state's development should not only be focused on mega projects, but needed to be translated through the availability of basic amenities that directly benefit the people such as affordable housing, schools, stalls, community halls and other public facilities.
"There is a big difference in terms of development between Johor Baru, Ulu Kempas, for instance Ulu Tebrau. That's just half an hour's (distance).
"There's no need for a 30-storey building in Ulu Tebrau, it's not necessary. But take care of schools, make sure there are halls and mosques," he said during his speech at the 'Kita Geng Madani Johor: Malam Anak Muda Kempas' programme here last night.
Anwar, who is also finance minister, said efforts to achieve more balanced development require the support of the people in electing leaders who are courageous, decisive and capable of representing their voices and aspirations.
"We need to elect young leaders and candidates who carry the message and aspirations of the people. Only in this way can we properly shape the future of Johor," he said.
He also shared his aspiration of seeing Malaysia become a strong and respected nation before he concludes his tenure as prime minister.
"I want Malaysia to be a strong nation, a great country, and for its people to be well looked after," he said.
Touching on the 16th Johor state election, Anwar reiterated the government's commitment to ensuring that the electoral process would be conducted in a free and fair manner.
He said the government would never interfere in the affairs of the Election Commission.
"This is what we must safeguard so that our country continues to be respected and succeeds as a great nation," he said.
Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Kempas state seat, Faezuddin Puad and PKR Youth chief Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim were present at the event.
A total of 172 candidates are contesting 56 seats this election, with polling set for July 11 and early voting scheduled for July 7.--BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Egypt expects €1.5bil from EU assistance package in coming days, minister says
Article:
DUBAI: Egypt expects to receive €1.5 billion (US$1.72 billion) from the European Union in the coming days, the first of two remaining tranches of a €5 billion macro-financial assistance package, Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty said on Saturday.
Speaking at a press conference in Egypt's new administrative capital alongside European Commissioner for the Mediterranean Dubravka Suica, Abdelatty said the outstanding €3 billion would be disbursed in two equal tranches of €1.5 billion each.
He said Cairo hoped the last payment would be transferred by the start of the autumn.
The EU has so far disbursed €2 billion of the package, having transferred an initial €1 billion tranche in January 2025 and a second €1 billion earlier this year.
The macro-financial assistance forms part of a broader €7.4 billion funding deal the EU announced in 2024, which also includes €5 billion in concessional loans.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: First-time voters urged to consider BN's track record
Article:
JOHOR BARU: First-time voters have been told to evaluate  Barisan Nasional's  track record in developing  the country.
Johor Umno deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan said since independence, the BN government had brought prosperity and harmony to the nation.
He said this commitment was ongoing in  efforts to achieve developed nation status.
"The BN government has always brought development, progress, prosperity and stability to the people and  nation.
"Schools, universities, colleges and skills institutions were mostly built by the BN government.
"Therefore,  continue to support  BN candidates so that development, progress, prosperity, and stability in Johor can continue," he said.
Earlier, Ahmad  and BN Larkin candidate Mohd Hairi Mad Shah campaigned in Taman Sinaran Kempas.
Hairi is  Umno Youth vice-chief. He will face Pulai member of parliament Suhaizan Kaiat of Pakatan Harapan and Bersama candidate Norsinah Abu.
The Larkin seat, created in 2018, was first won by Izhar Ahmad of Bersatu before Umno reclaimed it in the 2022 state election through Hairi.
The constituency has 76,662 voters.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia poised to extend uptrend, eyes 1,700 level next week
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's benchmark index is expected to extend its recovery next week and test the psychological 1,700-point level, underpinned by Friday's strong rebound and continued buying from local institutional investors.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said improving global risk sentiment is likely to provide a favourable backdrop for regional equities, including Bursa Malaysia.
"The combination of lower geopolitical risks and a potentially more accommodative monetary policy environment has helped improve appetite for risk assets globally," he told Bernama.
However, he said that a sustained break above this milestone will require broader market participation, particularly from heavyweight banking stocks, alongside continued domestic institutional support and a return of foreign buying.
"Stronger corporate earnings guidance and improving global market sentiment will also be important catalysts to support a more durable rally," he added.
For the week just ended, Bursa Malaysia ended mostly lower as late-session profit-taking intensified, reflecting investors' cautious sentiment amid the absence of fresh market catalysts.
On a Friday-to-Friday basis, the key index advanced 11.31 points to 1,679.05 from 1,667.74 a week earlier.
On the index board, the FBM Emas Index jumped 91.99 points to 12,457.01, the FBMT 100 Index widened 81.71 points to 12,283.30, and the FBM Emas Shariah Index climbed 50.93 points to 12,292.23.
The FBM Mid 70 Index surged 114.17 points to 17,840.36 and the FBM ACE Index soared 149.31 points to 4,894.61.
By sector, the Plantation Index rose 86.56 points to 8,969.54, the Energy Index gained 12.70 points to 758.60, the Financial Services Index garnered 168.84 points to 19,831.96, and the Industrial Products and Services Index perked up 2.85 points to 182.44.
Weekly turnover decreased to 13.63 billion units worth RM11.11 billion from 15.54 billion units worth RM13.11 billion a week earlier.
The Main Market volume declined to 6.73 billion units valued at RM9.72 billion against 7.78 billion units valued at RM11.58 billion previously.
Warrants turnover trimmed to 4.29 billion units worth RM529.34 million versus 5.15 billion units worth RM628.56 million last week.
The ACE Market volume increased marginally to 2.60 billion units valued at RM860.50 million compared with 2.59 billion units valued at RM899.90 million in the previous week.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: The choice between continuity and change
Article:
JOHOR BARU: One week in, the Johor election campaign  is shaping up to be a familiar choice between continuity and change, with Barisan Nasional  maintaining a visible advantage as the July 11 polling day  draws nearer.
The  campaign  began on June 29.
BN's messaging  centres on state governance, economic development and leadership credibility, allowing the  coalition to project administrative stability and continuity.
No major controversy has surfaced to shift voter sentiment. No single issue has dominated the discourse and no  momentum shift has been recorded in favour of opposition blocs seeking a check-and-balance mandate.
Sporadic political noise, including claims by former Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi,  did not develop into a  flashpoint.
The campaign  remained focused  on bread-and-butter issues, including cost of living pressures, jobs, affordable housing, flood mitigation, traffic congestion and expectations from  the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone.
For BN, the strategy has been straightforward: campaign on its track record.
Caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi remains the coalition's key figure, with candidates highlighting investment inflows, infrastructure delivery, welfare programmes and Johor's  economic performance.
BN has positioned itself as seeking a renewed mandate to continue policies  in motion — a message that appears to resonate  in suburban and semi-rural areas where delivery often outweighs political rhetoric.
Pakatan Harapan, meanwhile, has pursued a  grassroots-driven campaign, focusing on walkabouts, ceramah and direct voter engagement.
Its candidates have pushed themes of transparency, accountability and institutional reform, alongside pledges to ease financial pressures on households.
However, PH has yet to articulate a  statewide narrative convincing enough to displace an administration widely viewed by voters as stable and functional.
PH chairman and Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's campaign appearances have largely reinforced federal-state alignment and broader economic reform agendas under the Mada-ni framework.
Yet these themes have not translated into a strong electoral pull in Johor, where local issues continue to dominate voter priorities.
The opposition's check-and-balance message has also struggled to elevate national concerns, such as inflation and subsidy rationalisation, into decisive voting factors.
PH has not named a menteri besar candidate, maintaining that the prerogative lies with the Johor palace.
However, political discussions on the ground have frequently pointed to Dr Maszlee Malik as an informal front-runner.
While well-known and generally approachable, questions persist about  his executive experience in state administration.
Perikatan Nasional, which made notable gains in previous elections, has  failed to generate similar momentum.
Multi-cornered fights involving Pas, Bersatu, Muda, Parti Sosialis Malaysia, independents and smaller parties have  fragmented the opposition vote in several constituencies, a pattern that  could benefit other candidates.
Isolated campaign incidents, including allegations of vandalism of flags and banners,  have not escalated into broader  issues.
Voter turnout remains a key variable. The expanded electorate under the automatic registration and Undi18 initiative has brought in a significant number of younger voters, many of whom are politically fluid and focused on jobs, housing, wages and economic opportunity rather than party lines.
Whether they turn out in force could prove decisive in closely contested seats.
For now, independent surveys and observers continue to place BN in a leading position, although more than 30 seats remain competitive due to undecided voters and fragmented contests.
As the campaign enters its final stretch, all parties are expected to intensify their outreach efforts through ceramah,  ground mobilisation and social media campaigns  to sway fence-sitters.
Unless there is a major political disruption, the current trajectory suggests Johor voters are, at least for now, leaning towards stability and continuity over calls for  change.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Main Washington July Fourth event evacuated over storm threat
Article:
WASHINGTON: A massive July Fourth celebration on Washington's National Mall was evacuated on Saturday, due to an approaching severe thunderstorm, AFP reporters said.
A few hours before President Donald Trump was due to deliver a campaign rally-style address to a huge crowd, fierce winds buffeted the grounds and lightning flashed nearby, prompting officials to order people to immediately seek shelter.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Calls grow for national sex offender registry after Shah Alam case [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Calls are growing louder for Malaysia to establish a national sex offender registry and tighten safety measures at educational institutions following the arrest of a college employee in Shah Alam over the alleged sexual abuse of two 15-year-old boys.
Parent groups said it was high time the government took their demands for a national sex offender registry seriously if they are concerned about protecting children from predators.
They were commenting on the arrest of a 38-year-old suspect, with a  record of  child sexual assault  in 2023, at a restaurant in Shah Alam shortly after midnight yesterday.
Police are investigating allegations that the suspect molested a 15-year-old boy in a hostel toilet and sodomised another at his house in Section 18, Shah Alam.
SK La Salle Parent-Teacher Association chairman Leben Kuppusamy described the incident as a painful reminder that the country's  child protection safeguards were inadequate.
"It is alarming that yet another case involving an alleged child sexual offender, who was able to remain within society despite serious allegations against him, has surfaced.
"Every such incident is a painful reminder that our current safeguards are not enough."
He called on  the government to establish a comprehensive and legally robust national sexual offender registry to enable schools, childcare centres, tuition centres, religious institutions, sports academies and organisations working with children to conduct proper background checks before hiring employees or accepting volunteers.
"Protecting children must never be left to chance.
"At present, employers and schools have no practical or standardised mechanism to determine whether an applicant has a history of sexual offences against children.
"This creates a dangerous gap that allows offenders to move from one workplace or community to another unnoticed."
He said every individual seeking employment or volunteer positions involving children should undergo mandatory criminal background screening, with organisations legally required to verify the suitability of those entrusted with children's welfare.
Leben said stronger screening alone was insufficient, saying all institutions working with minors should implement comprehensive child safety policies.
"Staff members, volunteers and parents should receive regular training to recognise grooming behaviour, respond appropriately to disclosures and report suspected abuse immediately.
"As a nation, we cannot continue reacting only after children have been harmed.
"The safety of every child must become a national priority, backed by laws, systems and accountability."
SJKT PJS 1 Parent-Teacher Association vice-chairman Puwa-neswary Muniandy said the latest case should serve as a "massive wake-up call" for schools and parents.
"The most shocking detail is that the suspect already had a prior criminal record for child sexual assault.
"I believe we must take immediate action to protect our children.
"Academic success means nothing if our schools are not 100 per cent safe."
She said background vetting should extend beyond teachers to include everyone with access to children.
"Vetting is the only way to stop repeat offenders from getting jobs near our children. It cannot be limited to teachers.
"It must also include wardens, security guards, cleaners, canteen operators and school bus drivers."
She said schools must work more closely with police to verify the background of employees through the police database before appointments were made.
Puwaneswary called on  schools and colleges to strengthen physical security by addressing blind spots on campus.
"We need better CCTV coverage around toilets, changing rooms and isolated hostel areas where students may be vulnerable.
"Our message is clear: student safety is non-negotiable. We will accept nothing less than complete protection."
Two months ago, the
New Straits Times
front-paged a report about the arrest of 117 child predators.
Following the report, non-governmental organisations  and stakeholders called on  the government to consider setting up a public child offenders list.
In 2019, the Women, Family and Community Development Ministry set up the Sexual Crime Registry System, which contained the names of 3,000 offenders convicted for sexual offences against children.
Then deputy women, family and community development minister Hannah Yeoh had said the
Sexual Crime Registry System
was managed by the Welfare Department.
The list, however, was not made public as it required consultation with other ministries.
Yeoh  said to access the database, applicants had to fill up a  form stating their purpose and provide the identity card number of the person being checked.
She said if an application was done at a state Welfare Department office, the results could be obtained immediately.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Subsidise community gyms where green spaces have disappeared, say residents
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Federal and state governments and local councils should subsidise community gyms in urban areas to offset the loss of green spaces and help tackle rising lifestyle-related diseases, said two residents groups.
Bukit Bandaraya Residents Association adviser Datuk M. Ali and Petaling Jaya SS20 Rukun Tetangga chairman James Lee said communities would benefit from greater access to affordable neighbourhood gyms.
"When city planners preach about healthy lifestyles, they do so while simultaneously taking away our green lungs for development," Ali told the
New Sunday Times.
"Since urban living has deprived people of recreational and health facilities, introducing more community gyms would be highly effective."
He said local authorities should work with gym operators to establish affordable community gyms in neighbourhoods and People's Housing Projects.
[[nid:1480657]]
By providing adequate and reasonably priced facilities within public housing schemes, he said, the government could support local communities while steering youths towards healthier and more constructive activities.
Lee said community gyms were  beneficial for residents seeking to remain active.
"We have an ageing population in SS20. Having a smaller gym nearby is the right way to go."
He said affordable membership fees gave working adults and older residents a convenient alternative without having to travel to shopping malls, where most franchise gyms are located.
Earlier this year, Kuala Lumpur City Hall reduced annual licence fees for community gyms from RM50 to RM10 per square metre.
The Gymnasium Entrepreneurs Association Malaysia urged the Selangor government to introduce a similar reduction.
Lee said the Selangor government should step in to support community gym operators.
"Community gyms provide a vital public service. The state government should consider lowering licensing fees so more operators can serve local comm-unities."
Cheras resident Christopher Daniel Philips, 54, said he previously frequented the community gym at Bangsar Sports Complex, where entry cost just RM5.
He said distance remained the biggest obstacle.
"If the gym were closer to home, I would  go more often."
[[nid:1480656]]
Gym-goer Ahmad, 28, said his no-frills neighbourhood gym offered everything he needed for just RM10 per visit.
"The gym is next to my apartment. I just walk downstairs, take a few steps and I'm there.
"These small gyms are crucial to building a health-conscious society. People who come here are committed because they genuinely want to look after their health, not show off."
He said local councils could make fitness more accessible by lowering licensing fees and supporting aspiring community gym operators.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gym group wants lower licensing in Selangor [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Gymnasium Entrepreneurs Association Malaysia  has urged the Selangor government to follow Kuala Lumpur's lead by reducing annual licensing fees, saying the move would help make fitness more accessible.
Its president K. Krishnakumar said community gyms — independently owned facilities commonly located in neighbourhood shoplots — primarily serve lower- and middle-income Malaysians.
He said operators were seeking lower annual licensing fees in Selangor, where rates vary between local authorities.
On average, gym owners are charged RM50 per square metre.
For Krishnakumar's gym, this works out to around RM11,000 per year for a 220sq m space.
"Some local councils require double deposits, forcing new operators to pay twice the standard amount just to obtain a licence," Krishnakumar said.
He said the association would write to the Selangor government to seek a meeting on the matter.
He said Kuala Lumpur City Hall   reduced annual licence fees from RM50 to RM10 per square metre earlier this year following the intervention of Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh.
"Previously, City Hall classified community gyms under the broad category of wellness centres, placing them in the same licensing bracket as luxury spas and high-end massage parlours."
He said lower licensing fees in Malaysia's most populous state had become increasingly important as more Malaysians sought healthier lifestyles to prevent lifestyle-related diseases.
The National Health and Morbidity Survey 2024 found that 53.6 per cent of Malaysian adults were overweight or obese, while one in three adults was physically inactive.
Krishnakumar, whose association represents 380 members operating about 620 community gyms nationwide, said affordable fitness was key to improving public health.
Community gyms typically charge monthly membership fees of between RM85 and RM110,
with walk-in rates from RM9 per visit, compared with fitness chains that can charge up to RM300 a month.
He said association members had recorded year-on-year growth of up to 20 per cent over the past five years, driven largely by rising memberships among women and young people.
"If we can keep operating costs low, community gyms will be better placed to help more Malaysians lead healthier lives."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Prayers to be offered for Iran's Khamenei on day two of funeral ceremonies
Article:
TEHERAN: Prayers were to be said over the casket of slain Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei on Sunday, the second day of marathon funeral ceremonies that have already drawn huge crowds to pay their final respects in Teheran.
Authorities have not announced whether a religious figure or a family member will lead the prayers for Khamenei, who ruled Iran from 1989 until he was killed aged 86 in an airstrike on the first day of the Middle East war on February 28.
His son and successor Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since being named supreme leader, and is said to have been wounded in the same attack.
Sunday has been declared a public holiday for the entire country, and in the evening, Khamenei's body will leave the Grand Mosalla complex where it is lying in state in preparation for processions through the capital planned for Monday.
Hassan Hassanzadeh, in charge of the ceremonies as commander of the powerful Revolutionary Guards in Teheran, said that "prayers will be carried out over the holy body of the martyred leader" at 8am local time (0430 GMT), according to state TV.
On Saturday, vast crowds of Iranians loyal to the Islamic republic had massed for the start of the public funeral ceremonies, which authorities hope will serve as a message of defiance after the war with the US and Israel.
Clad in black and waving blood-red flags symbolising vengeance and justice, mourners beat their chests in a sign of grief as chants of "death to America" and "revenge, revenge" echoed around the venue.
"The leader was a father to us all. With his passing, we have all been left orphaned," said Mohammad Mirsalehi, 38, a cleric.
The coffins of Khamenei, with his black turban on top, and four family members also killed in the February 28 strikes – including his infant granddaughter – were placed at the front on a raised dais, AFP photographs showed.
Authorities believe the ceremonies will mobilise more than 10 million people in the capital alone.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RMN adopts 'firm but measured' stance to safeguard maritime sovereignty
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Royal Malaysian Navy  is taking a measured, non-aggressive military posture in asserting Malaysia's maritime sovereignty by working  with other security agencies to address threats at sea.
Its chief, Admiral Tan Sri Zulhelmy Ithnain, told the
New Sunday Times
that this was particularly important in sensitive maritime areas such as the South China Sea.
"We are tackling  threats through coordinated operations and a whole-of-government framework in maritime presence, deterrence and escalation management.
"In so-called grey-zone (non-military strategies involving subtle challenges) situations where enforcement responsibilities are shared, the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) takes the lead in enforcement action, while the navy provides support through maritime presence, deterrence and escalation management," he said.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin had recently said the country will continue to safeguard its sovereignty, sovereign rights and strategic interests in the South China Sea.
Earlier this year, a mid-term review of the Defence White Paper highlighted that rising tensions in the South China Sea could increase the risk of unintended military clashes, as the presence of major power warships and intensified military aircraft operations continued to escalate the situation.
Khaled had said  the use of
grey-zone tactics  such as hydrographic surveys escorted by foreign coast guard vessels in Malaysia's Exclusive Economic Zone   provoked and undermined national sovereignty.
Zulhelmy said operations such as Op Naga and Op Guard had demonstrated the effectiveness of this integrated approach in addressing maritime threats, including illegal fishing, smuggling activities and human trafficking.
"These threats are increasingly transnational in nature, often involving organised networks and more sophisticated methods of operation.
"The navy remains focused on ensuring maritime security, protecting economic interests and safeguarding sea lines of communication, while continuing to work alongside enforcement agencies," he said.
On regional maritime security cooperation, Zulhelmy said the navy was strengthening interoperability with strategic partners through exercises conducted domestically, regionally and internationally.
"This year, the navy will continue to participate in a structured exercise programme to ensure fleet readiness remains at the highest level."
Domestically, he said, intra-navy exercises such as Exercise Kerismas and Ostex are aimed at enhancing operational capability and combat preparedness, while inter-service exercises such as Exercise Pahlawan are designed to strengthen jointness and interoperability in the armed forces.
Regionally, he said, the navy maintains five bilateral exercises on a biennial basis with Asean countries, alongside participation in exercises under broader regional frameworks involving  China, Japan, the United States and India.
He added that the navy also takes part in multilateral exercises hosted by Indonesia and the Philippines.
Internationally, he said, the navy continues to engage in exercises such as Exercise Kakadu with the Royal Australian Navy, Exercise Milan with the Indian Navy, as well as bilateral drills with Pakistan, Japan and France, all of which reinforce regional maritime security cooperation and interoperability with strategic partners.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Putin, Zelensky speak to Trump ahead of NATO summit
Article:
MOSCOW: US President Donald Trump spoke with his Russian and Ukrainian counterparts Vladimir Putin and Volodymyr Zelensky Saturday to discuss the conflict in Ukraine ahead of a NATO summit in Ankara, the Kremlin and Zelensky said.
The calls were both to mark the 250th birthday of the United States, both sides said.
"The presidents naturally addressed the issue of a settlement in Ukraine, taking into account, in particular, Donald Trump's upcoming participation in the NATO summit in Turkey on July 7 and 8," said Yuri Ushakov, the Kremlin's foreign policy aide, according to the RIA Novosti news agency.
He said the two leaders also discussed issues including Iran and the Middle East during the 85-minute call.
Earlier Saturday, Zelensky said he and Trump had also discussed the more than four years of war in his country, calling it a "very good phone call".
"President Trump and I discussed the current situation on the frontline as well as our diplomatic efforts. There is a real prospect to put an end to this war, and America's resolve is decisive. We have agreed to continue these discussions during the NATO Summit in Ankara," he wrote on X.
Heads of state and delegations from 32 countries, including Trump, are expected to arrive in Ankara starting Tuesday for the summit.
Putin "outlined the actual situation on the battlefield (in Ukraine), where Russian armed forces are advancing confidently," stated Ushakov.
On the front lines, Russian forces have made almost no progress in recent months, largely due to the presence of drones, which hinder the movement of heavy vehicles and inflict heavy losses on both sides.
Diplomatically, negotiations are at an impasse, with Moscow demanding the withdrawal of Ukrainian forces from the entire Donetsk region -- a demand Kyiv rejects.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US VP Vance says Britain has been failed by leaders, hopes next PM delivers change
Article:
LONDON: US Vice President JD Vance said Britain had been "failed by its leadership for a long time," adding that he hoped the country's next prime minister could deliver the structural change voters were seeking after years of political turmoil.
In an interview with the Sunday Times newspaper, Vance said frequent changes of government pointed to deeper problems in British politics.
Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced last month he would step down after two years in office, paving the way for Britain's seventh prime minister in a decade.
Lawmaker Andy Burnham is widely expected to succeed Starmer after emerging as the sole candidate to replace the outgoing leader.
"What I see is six prime ministers in the last few years," Vance told the newspaper.
"What that says to me is that something is very broken about British politics and that people are really crying out for significant structural change."
"I hope that Andy Burnham — and if not Andy Burnham, somebody else — is able to deliver it," the vice president said, adding that "whoever the prime minister is figures out how to get Britain back on track."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ukraine attacks St Petersburg, says Russian seizure of key town a 'lie'
Article:
UKRAINE: Ukraine denied Saturday Moscow's announcement that Russian forces had captured the strategic Ukrainian city of Kostyantynivka, a stronghold for Kyiv's troops in the eastern Donetsk region and a key target for the Kremlin.
A Ukrainian army spokesman told AFP the city remained under its control, while President Volodymyr Zelensky dismissed Moscow's announcement as "a lie", with the duelling claims coming after Russia also said Ukrainian drones had struck its second city, St Petersburg.
"The situation remains difficult," Ukrainian army spokesman Andriy Kovalyov told AFP, but Kostyantynivka "is under the control of the Ukrainian Defence Forces."
On Friday, Russian President Vladimir Putin, wearing a military uniform, had thanked troops for seizing Kostyantynivka, a town with a pre-war population of 78,000 that Moscow has been trying to take control over for months.
The army has been sending in small groups of soldiers to infiltrate and secure positions, a method previously used to capture the Ukrainian city of Pokrovsk in late 2025.
Kostyantynivka is one of the final obstacles on the route to the major Ukrainian-held cities of Kramatorsk and Sloviansk; capturing them is the Kremlin's ultimate goal in the Donbas.
Kovalyov said on Saturday that "Ukrainian defenders continue to hold their positions along the designated defensive lines."
He admitted that small groups of Russian troops have been infiltrating the town but insisted that fighting was ongoing.
Moscow's defence ministry, however, said: "Russian troops are in all parts of the town – from the southern to northern outskirts."
Zelensky called the claim "just another Russian lie."
"If Kostyantynivka were under Russian control, then perhaps Putin would have no problem meeting me there to find a diplomatic way to finally end this war," the Ukrainian leader said on social media.
Putin has repeatedly turned down talks with Zelensky to end more than four years of war, saying Moscow intends to capture the rest of eastern Ukraine by force.
Also on Saturday, Russia's defence ministry proposed Kyiv halt shelling in Kostyantynivka on Monday between 0900 and 1500 GMT to carry out a "humanitarian" operation to hand over "the bodies of killed Ukrainian soldiers."
- St Petersburg hit -
The claims came as Russia said Saturday Ukrainian drones hit an oil terminal in Saint Petersburg, Putin's hometown, as well as striking a port near Finland and falling on the historical Peterhof complex without causing damage.
Moscow vowed to respond, saying it downed almost 500 Ukrainian drones and 10 of Kyiv's Flamingo missiles overnight.
The Ukrainian attack came after a Russian strike on Kyiv this week killed 30 people, amid other strikes.
Zelensky also claimed Kyiv struck the Kronstadt naval base in Saint Petersburg.
Ukraine has been increasing strikes on Russian territory – hitting as far as the Urals far away from the front line – in recent months, in retaliation for Moscow's dragging offensive.
It is also a bid to bring Russia to the negotiating table, more than four years into its offensive that turned into Europe's worst war since WWII.
Leningrad region governor Alexander Drozdenko said the port of Vysotsk, near the border with NATO-member Finland, had been hit.
"Debris has been reported in the area of the port of Vysotsk," Drozdenko said on social media early Saturday, while saying the "attack was still being repelled."
Saint Petersburg governor Alexander Beglov said drones hit "the area of an oil terminal in the Kirovsk district" of Saint Petersburg.
"Air defence forces shot down 72 drones, one of which crashed in Peterhof. There were no casualties or damage," he added.
The Peterhof palace is a giant estate of gardens and a palace built in the 18th century under Russian Tsar Peter I.
- Life disruptions -
Moscow Mayor Sergei Sobyanin said air defence had shot down 62 drones headed for the Russian capital.
Moscow said one person was killed in the border Bryansk region and another in Russian-annexed Crimea.
Authorities in the border Belgorod region said "infrastructure facilities" had been damaged by the attacks, leading to "disruptions to electricity and water supply."
Ukraine's increasing drone strikes on Russian cities have affected ordinary life in Russia more than at any other point during the conflict.
In Russia's Kaliningrad exclave, there were delays at the airport taking passengers to mainland Russia due to the strikes, according to an AFP reporter there.
Several Russian regions have also experienced fuel shortages, causing hours-long queues for petrol, due to Ukraine hitting oil and fuel depots.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tens of thousands rally in Albania against Trump-linked resort project
Article:
ALBANIA: Tens of thousands rallied on Saturday in the Albanian capital Tirana in the largest protest since a movement against a resort linked to US President Donald Trump's family began.
The 35th consecutive daily protest was the biggest since rallies started in late May against the planned construction of a luxury hotel linked to Trump's daughter Ivanka and her husband Jared Kushner in a nature reserve on the Balkan country's coast, AFP reporters said.
Opposition to the project has become a flashpoint for frustrations over perceived corruption, with protesters calling for Prime Minister Edi Rama to step down over what they describe as a lack of transparency.
The movement was dubbed the Flamingo Revolution as the pink birds migrate to a nature reserve where the project is planned.
"What began as the 'Flamingo Revolution' has sparked widespread public discontent," protester Alketa Ademi told AFP.
"Lack of transparency, arrogance – enough is enough! The prime minister has to leave," the 40-year-old said.
Protesters say the estimated $4.6-billion luxury hotel development slated for construction in Zvernec poses a risk to the environment and to a nearby lagoon critical to migrating birds.
Developers also hope to transform the uninhabited island of Sazan – once a secret communist military base – into a glitzy tourist destination.
- 'Albania not for sale' -
The protests are held daily in Tirana, while on Thursday morning for a second time this week, demonstrators gathered outside parliament to confront lawmakers and block entry to the building.
Police used tear gas, pepper spray and water cannon to break up hundreds of demonstrators, some of whom tried to push through lines of officers, threw eggs, stones and other objects.
Fifteen police officers were injured, while 25 demonstrators were detained, according to the police.
The Albanian Helsinki Committee, a leading local rights group, earlier Saturday voiced "concern about the escalation of the situation" at the protest.
"Individual violence cannot justify the disproportionate use of force" by the police, it said.
The police used tear gas without warning and unnecessary force including batons and punches against neutralised protesters on the ground, the watchdog warned calling for a swift and independent investigation.
Thursday's violence was a marked contrast to the overwhelmingly peaceful daily gatherings that have drawn thousands to the streets since the movement began.
"Free the boys," the demonstrators chanted on Saturday evening referring to 19 protesters still detained at a Tirana police station.
They carried banners reading "Albania is not for sale" and "Revoke law on protected areas" referring to the legislation that has  allowed the government to fast-track the project.
Some protesters carried giant pink flamingos through the crowd that marched to the government headquarters on Tirana's main boulevard.
A group of demonstrators carried a large cake made of concrete, symbolically marking Rama's birthday as the prime minister turns 62 on Saturday.
A number of Albanians from the diaspora have returned to Tirana to join the demonstrations, including Xheku Shena from Canada.
"We are not against foreign investment, we are for dignity, respect, and transparency, and against the current model that is harmful to the public interest," she told AFP.
The planned resort was first unveiled in 2024, but the latest wave of protests began after barbed-wire fencing and bulldozers appeared on beaches in late May.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ukraine's Zelenskiy says he spoke to Trump, calls for 'American resolve' to help end war
Article:
UKRAINE: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said on Saturday that he had spoken to US President Donald Trump on the Fourth of July holiday and called for "American resolve" to help end the more than four-year-old war with Russia.
Zelenskiy said he had a "very good" conversation with Trump, discussing the situation on the front line.
"There is a real prospect to end this war and American resolve will have a crucial meaning," he said.
Zelenskiy said he and Trump had agreed to "continue our conversation" at next week's NATO summit in Turkiye.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump says Netanyahu 'knows who the boss is': Axios
Article:
WASHINGTON: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu could visit the White House as early as next week, US President Donald Trump told Axios on Saturday, adding that the prime minister "knows who the boss is" in their relationship.
The possible meeting comes amid reports of tensions between the two leaders over efforts to end the war against Iran that the US and Israel started in late February.
"We get along very good. (Netanyahu) knows who the boss is," Trump said in a brief phone interview with Axios, referring to himself.
An Israeli official told the US media outlet that next week might be too soon for the visit because Trump will be traveling to Turkey for a NATO summit on July 7-8.
"It might take place the week after," the official told Axios.
Netanyahu's office said the two men spoke on Friday and agreed to meet "soon" in the United States.
Washington is Israel's closest ally, but Trump has been publicly critical of Netanyahu in recent weeks after Israel's war with Hezbollah in Lebanon threatened peace talks with Iran.
The two men last met on Feb 11 in Washington.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man climbs Eiffel Tower, hangs US flag on America's big birthday
Article:
PARIS:  A man climbed the Eiffel Tower on Saturday and draped the US flag on Paris's emblematic landmark on the 250th anniversary of American independence, police said.
The man was spotted in the afternoon climbing the tower between the second and third floors and hanging the Stars and Stripes.
He was arrested and the forecourt, as well as the second and third floors, were evacuated for a while.
The climber gave no explanation for his motives, police said.
The flag was taken down.
Seven million tourists pay to see the 324-metre (1,063-foot) tower each year.
Unveiled in 1889 for the World Fair in Paris by engineer Gustave Eiffel, the "Iron Lady" has since become the French capital's symbol.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran envoy says friendly nations to get 'special' Hormuz fee treatment
Article:
BEIJING: Iran's ambassador to China insisted Saturday that new fees would be charged to ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz – an idea rejected by Washington – while assuring that "friendly" nations would receive special treatment.
The initial deal struck between Iran and the United States to end their war stipulated that commercial ships would transit the strait free of charge for 60 days, but it remains unclear what will be in place after that period.
Iranian Ambassador Abdolreza Rahmani Fazli told the World Peace Forum in Beijing that his country was working in "collaboration and cooperation" with Oman on "new arrangements" for the vital waterway.
"As a country where the Hormuz is part of its territorial waters, we will definitely charge service fees," Azli said in translated remarks, while insisting such fees would not be a "toll."
"These new arrangements will be concerning guaranteeing the security of passage through the Straits of Hormuz, supervision of the passage of the vessels... and also guaranteeing and dealing with the environmental consequences of the massive number of ships," he said.
"We will definitely consider special treatment for the countries that were friendly to us and specially stood by us during the hard times," he added.
The strait normally carries one-fifth of the world's crude oil and liquefied natural gas, but it was all but closed by Iran during the Middle East war, sending energy prices soaring.
Iran lifted its blockade of the Hormuz after it struck an initial deal with the US to end the Middle East war, and negotiations on a permanent settlement of the conflict are ongoing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar urges Johor to prioritise people’s needs over mega projects such as JS‑SEZ and RTS
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim called on the Johor state government to address the people’s needs rather than focusing on the state’s mega projects.
He said a state is not measured only by mega projects, but also the needs of the people.
Anwar reminded that state governments that they have a responsibility to improve public housing, schools, mosques, roads and facilities in the villages and also in Felda schemes.
“There is no point complaining that the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) blueprint is delayed and the project is running with tens of billions of ringgit inflows recorded.
“The project had already attracted around RM10 billion in investments even before the agreement was signed.
“The negotiations were also conducted directly by me and my Singapore counterpart,” he said when speaking at the Johor Youth Dialogue programme in Felda Ulu Tebrau near here last night.
Anwar attended the event in his capacity as the Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman and PKR president. He was accompanied by the coalition’s Puteri Wangsa candidate Dr Maszlee Malik.
He said Johor was receiving substantial federal allocation because of its potential as an important economic growth catalyst in the region.
“In fact, Johor’s allocation is higher than those given to Perak, Selangor and Penang.” he said.
Anwar pointed out that major development projects such as the JS-SEZ and Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link are under the responsibility of the federal government to complete.
Besides large-scale projects, he said the state government should look into improving facilities under its jurisdiction such as small roads, public housing, mosques and also rural facilities.
“Please take a look. I will help with the (large-scale projects) worth tens of billions as that is my responsibility,” he said.
An the event, Anwar said engaging in dialogue and defending fact-based views reflects on society’s maturity in dealing with differences of opinion.
He said that the culture of dialogue is the best medium to build understanding, give rise to ideas and be an effective solution to various issues.
“An open dialogue can also strengthen unity and foster a culture of mutual respect despite differences of opinion within the community,” he said.

Title: Former Umno stalwart Puad makes surprise appearance at Pakatan event, declares support for coalition
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 5 — Former Umno supreme council member Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi made a surprise appearance at a Pakatan Harapan (PH) youth event here last night.
He arrived at the Johor Youth Dialogue programme venue in Felda Ulu Tebrau at 9.40pm and was then greeted by PH chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
In his speech, the former Johor state assembly speaker and Rengit assemblyman said he attended the programme to fulfil a friend’s invitation.
On his decision to campaign for PH, Mohd Puad explained that he has since left Umno and is an old friend of Anwar.
“My attendance here tonight was also to express my support for PH’s Puteri Wangsa candidate Maszlee Malik,” said the outspoken 68-year-old politician
Earlier, Mohd Puad had openly declared his support with a campaign poster for PH’s Rengit candidate, Mohamad Yazid Bakri, for the Johor state election.
He said his decision was based on the candidate’s personality and not on partisan party lines.
Mohd Puad, once known as an Umno stalwart and staunch Barisan Nasional (BN) supporter, announced his exit from the party and its coalition on June 25.
Previously, he publicly accused caretaker Johor menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who is also the state’s BN chairman, for non-compliance and deviation when announcing the dissolution of the state assembly, paving the way for the state election.
Following that, Umno secretary-general Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki claimed that Mohd Puad had threatened to leave the party if his son was not nominated for the Rengit seat ahead of the state poll.
In the 2022 Johor state election, Mohd Puad won the Rengit state seat with a 1,920-majority vote, defeating three other candidates from Perikatan Nasional (PN), Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) and Parti Pejuang Tanah Air (Pejuang).
He was previously the Batu Pahat MP and was Deputy Minister of Education II from 2009 to 2013 during Datuk Seri Najib Razak’s BN administration.
In the 2013 general election, he was defeated by the then Pakatan Rakyat (PR) candidate from PKR, Mohd Idris Jusi.
Mohd Puad later came to national prominence as the Special Affairs Department (Jasa) director-general from 2015 until his resignation in 2018 after he was not fielded as the Batu Pahat parliamentary candidate.

Title: In Johor, Anwar says politicians shouting ‘Long live Malays’ should walk the talk
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today criticised certain parties that frequently champion Malay supremacy, while at the same time, allowing Malay reserve land to fall into the hands of others.
He expressed disappointment that the issue was often used as political capital to garner support and votes during elections, when defending the interests of the Malays should instead be demonstrated through transparent actions and policies.
“Do not merely shout Bumiputera or Malay, long live the Malays, when elections are near, only to seize Malay contracts, Malay projects and Malay assets after gaining power.
“I want to ask the parties that frequently speak about Malay supremacy, when was the last time you created Malay reserve land? What I know is that a great deal of Malay reserve land has been lost to others,” said Anwar.
He said this when speaking at a gathering with Johor youth in conjunction with the 2026 Johor-level Kembara Inspirasi Belia Akar Umbi (KIBAR) programme at Taman Melor, Tampoi here today.
Also present were Selangor Menteri Besar and Pakatan Harapan (PH) Johor State Election director, Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari, and Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari.
Anwar, who is also finance minister, said that as proof of his administration’s commitment, the government had, for the first time since independence, gazetted 20.23 hectares of strategic land in Bandar Malaysia, Kuala Lumpur, as Malay reserve land.
He said the Bandar Malaysia project, located at the former Sungai Besi Air Base, was expected to have a gross development value (GDV) of RM140 billion.
Anwar said the move demonstrated the government’s commitment to safeguarding the interests of Malays and Bumiputera without denying the rights of other communities to also benefit from national development.
According to him, the empowerment of Bumiputera entrepreneurs remains a priority through various concrete initiatives, including the launch of Perbadanan Usahawan Nasional Berhad (PUNB) SparK 2026: Business Transformation programme today.
Through the programme, PUNB is targeting loan approvals of up to RM2.25 billion from 2026 to 2030 to strengthen Bumiputera entrepreneurship development. — Bernama

Title: Teo Nie Ching calls for harmonious campaigning after Pakatan materials vandalised in several Johor seats
Article:
KULAI, July 4 — Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching has urged all parties to campaign in a harmonious manner after incidents of vandalism involving Pakatan Harapan (PH) campaign materials were reported in several areas in the 16th Johor state election.
